agent1_raw: The central flaw in this evaluation is label validity. The labels capture what dispatchers did under heterogeneous practices — not the underlying communication need. This means the model cannot be trusted to make prospective decisions about when to delay interpretation. Additionally, aggregate-only metrics provide no insight into safety for high-stakes call types. The override rule is a useful but imperfect safeguard. The retrospective evidence does not justify the deployment as framed.
agent2_raw: Two problems: (1) the model's training labels are policy artifacts, not measures of communication need, creating a counterfactual gap the retrospective data cannot bridge; (2) there are no stratified metrics by acuity or call type. The vendor's claim of minimal high-acuity impact is asserted without evidence. The override rule relies on behavioral proxies that may not catch all high-acuity situations. The retrospective evidence is insufficient to justify deployment.
agent3_raw: The proposed deployment involves a policy change (delay interpreter connection for low-risk calls), but the retrospective evaluation was conducted under the old policy (connect interpreters by default). This means the model was trained to predict outcomes under a regime that the deployment would eliminate. The training-deployment mismatch is fundamental. Combined with the absence of any call-type stratification, the current evidence cannot support the deployment decision. A prospective pilot with independent outcome adjudication is the minimum threshold.
synthesis_raw: All three agents converge on critique_wins. Core issues: (1) labels reflect historical policy rather than communication need; (2) no stratified analysis by call type or acuity. Agent 3 explicitly articulates the training-deployment mismatch as a counterfactual gap. No empirical test is needed to reach this conclusion — the retrospective evidence is structurally insufficient for the deployment decision. The ETD constraint check: no empirically resolvable genuine disagreement identified; critique_wins is the appropriate verdict.
all_issues_raised: Labels are policy artifacts under the old policy
No stratified analysis by call type or acuity
Training-deployment mismatch creates unbridgeable counterfactual gap
Override rule relies on imperfect behavioral proxies
